description: Automatic certificate rotation (Task Group 8: full implementation). Worker with 1-hour ticker, CheckExpiring, Regenerate, DistributeToNodes, cert.distribute task action in node agent, warning/critical events, and panel startup initialization.
steps: Create migration panel/migrations/024_cert_rotation.sql: This is a no-op since 017_cert_expiry.sql already added expires_at and fingerprint columns. Content: '-- Certificate rotation support (base columns already in 017_cert_expiry.sql)\nSELECT 1;'

Create panel/internal/certrotation/worker.go with package certrotation. Define: type Worker struct { db *sql.DB; interval time.Duration; stop chan struct{} }. func New(db *sql.DB) *Worker (sets interval to 1 hour). func (w *Worker) Start() - launches goroutine with time.Ticker that calls w.run() every hour. func (w *Worker) Stop(). func (w *Worker) run() - calls CheckExpiring then processes results.

Implement CheckExpiring(db *sql.DB) ([]ExpiringCert, error) that queries: SELECT id, name, cert_path, expires_at, fingerprint FROM vpn_certificates WHERE expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < NOW() + INTERVAL 30 DAY AND (status IS NULL OR status != 'revoked'). Returns slice of ExpiringCert{ID, Name, CertPath, ExpiresAt, Fingerprint, DaysUntilExpiry}.

Implement Regenerate(cert ExpiringCert) (newFingerprint string, err error) that: 1) determines cert type from path (CA vs server vs tls-crypt), 2) calls appropriate openssl command via exec.Command (e.g., 'openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 3650 -newkey ec -pkeyopt ec_paramgen_curve:prime256v1 ...' for CA, or 'openssl x509 -req ...' for server certs), 3) updates the vpn_certificates table with new expires_at and fingerprint, 4) returns the new fingerprint.

Implement DistributeToNodes(db *sql.DB, cert ExpiringCert) error that: 1) queries all online/stale nodes from the nodes table, 2) for each node, creates a node task with action='cert.distribute' and payload containing cert_path and cert_content (base64 encoded).

In the run() method, after CheckExpiring: for each cert, if DaysUntilExpiry <= 7, call createEvent with severity='error' (critical). If DaysUntilExpiry <= 30, call createEvent with severity='warning'. If DaysUntilExpiry <= 7, call Regenerate then DistributeToNodes. Store an eventCreator func in the Worker struct (func(eventType, severity, title, message string)) to decouple from api.Server.

In node/cmd/node/main.go executeTask switch, add case 'cert.distribute': reads cert_path and cert_content (base64) from payload, decodes content, writes to cert_path with 0600 permissions, then validates chain with 'openssl verify -CAfile /etc/openvpn/server/ca.crt <cert_path>' (if ca.crt exists). Return succeeded with validation result.

In panel/cmd/panel/main.go, after startWorker(database) call, add: certWorker := certrotation.New(database); certWorker.Start(). Add import 'koris-next/panel/internal/certrotation'. Pass an event creator function that uses the database to insert events.

Write unit tests in panel/internal/certrotation/worker_test.go: test that CheckExpiring returns correct results with mock data (test the SQL query structure), test that DaysUntilExpiry calculation is correct. Since we cannot run a real DB, test the helper functions (fingerprint calculation, cert type detection) in isolation.
acceptance_criteria: certrotation.Worker struct exists with Start/Stop methods
CheckExpiring queries certs expiring within 30 days
Regenerate calls openssl to regenerate certificates
DistributeToNodes creates cert.distribute tasks for online nodes
cert.distribute task action in node agent writes cert and validates with openssl verify
Panel main.go initializes cert rotation worker
Warning events generated for 30-day expiry, critical for 7-day
